questions.text: Which protocol provides encrypted communication over port 443?

explanations.a: HTTP uses port 80 and transmits data in plaintext — no encryption.
explanations.b: HTTPS uses TLS to encrypt data in transit and runs on port 443. This is the correct answer.
explanations.c: FTP uses ports 20/21 and is unencrypted by default.
explanations.d: Telnet uses port 23 and sends all data including credentials in plaintext.

questions.text: What does a firewall primarily do?

answers.text: Filters incoming and outgoing traffic based on rules

explanations.a: Encryption is handled by protocols like TLS/IPSec, not firewalls.
explanations.b: Firewalls inspect packets and allow or deny them based on configured rules. This is the correct answer.
explanations.c: IP address assignment is done by a DHCP server.
explanations.d: Malware scanning is performed by antivirus/endpoint protection software.

questions.text: Which port does DNS typically use?

explanations.a: Port 21 is used by FTP for control connections.
explanations.b: DNS operates on port 53 for both UDP (queries) and TCP (zone transfers). This is the correct answer.
explanations.d: Port 110 is used by POP3 for email retrieval.

questions.text: What is a DMZ (Demilitarized Zone) in network security?

answers.text: A zone with no network access at all
A subnet that exposes external-facing services while isolating the internal network
A VPN endpoint for remote users
A backup network segment for disaster recovery

explanations.a: A DMZ is not isolated — it is intentionally accessible from the internet.
explanations.b: A DMZ sits between the internet and internal network, hosting services like web servers while protecting internal resources. This is the correct answer.
explanations.c: VPN endpoints are separate infrastructure; a DMZ can contain one but is not defined by it.
explanations.d: Backup segments are separate; a DMZ serves live internet-facing services.

questions.text: What is the purpose of Network Address Translation (NAT)?

answers.text: To map private IP addresses to a public IP address
To assign domain names to IP addresses
To monitor network traffic for anomalies

explanations.a: NAT does not encrypt traffic; IPSec or TLS handles encryption.
explanations.b: NAT allows multiple devices on a private network to share one public IP address. This is the correct answer.
explanations.c: DNS maps domain names to IP addresses, not NAT.
explanations.d: Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) monitor traffic for anomalies.

questions.text: Which protocol is used to securely manage network devices remotely?

explanations.a: Telnet sends data in plaintext, making it insecure for remote management.
explanations.b: SNMP v1 lacks authentication and encryption — SNMPv3 is the secure version.
explanations.c: SSH (Secure Shell) provides encrypted remote management over port 22. This is the correct answer.
explanations.d: HTTP is unencrypted; HTTPS would be used for web-based management interfaces.

questions.text: What is the primary difference between a stateful and stateless firewall?

answers.text: Stateful firewalls are faster because they do not inspect packet headers
Stateless firewalls track active connections; stateful ones inspect each packet in isolation
Stateful firewalls track the state of active connections; stateless ones inspect each packet independently
Stateless firewalls use deep packet inspection; stateful ones do not

explanations.a: Stateful firewalls actually do more work — tracking connection tables, not less.
explanations.b: This reverses the definitions of stateful and stateless.
explanations.c: Stateful firewalls maintain connection state tables to allow return traffic; stateless firewalls evaluate each packet independently. This is the correct answer.
explanations.d: Deep packet inspection is a separate capability unrelated to stateful vs. stateless distinction.

questions.text: Which VPN protocol is considered the most secure for site-to-site connections?

explanations.a: PPTP uses weak MS-CHAPv2 authentication and is considered broken by modern standards.
explanations.b: L2TP alone provides no encryption; it requires IPSec for security.
explanations.c: IPSec tunnel mode encapsulates the entire IP packet, providing strong authentication and encryption for site-to-site VPNs. This is the correct answer.
explanations.d: SSTP is Microsoft-specific and primarily used for remote access, not site-to-site.
